Title 2 — Administration & Personnel Plain-Language Service Free · no account

A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 2 (Administration & Personnel) — how County government is organized: the departments, the boards and commissions (including the Planning Commission, Ch. 2.28), the General Plan framework (Ch. 2.80B) that guides community plans, and personnel rules. This is the title that turns the Charter into a working administration. For the full charter-to-department mapping, see the govOS Charter Hub. Sections marked expanding are being sourced verbatim; nothing here is legal advice.

1 · How the County is organized sourced

The administrative backbone every service runs on.

Title 2 (Administration & Personnel) sets up County government: the departments (Planning, Public Works, Finance, Water Supply, Environmental Management, Parks & Recreation, Transportation, Agriculture, and more), the boards and commissions that decide and advise, and the personnel/civil-service rules for County employees. It's where the Charter's structure becomes the agencies you actually deal with in every other title's service.

Maui County Code Title 2 (Administration & Personnel). full department & commission chapter list

2 · Key chapters sourced

The parts of Title 2 residents meet most often.

ChapterSubjectWhy it matters to you
2.28Planning Commission(s)Decides zoning permits, plan amendments, SMA (the body behind Title 19)
2.80BGeneral Plan / community plansThe long-range framework that guides community-plan land use
Boards & commissionsVariances & Appeals, Liquor, Ethics, etc.Where you appeal or testify
Departments & personnelDepartment powers; civil serviceWho administers each service

Maui County Code Title 2 — Ch. 2.28 (Planning Commission), Ch. 2.80B (General Plan), board/commission and personnel chapters, Municode. per-board membership & duties

3 · The lens — the Charter made administration analysis

Labeled analysis — county organization read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.

Maui County Charter — lensUnder the Maui County Charter, the Charter creates the offices and the broad structure, and Title 2 implements it — establishing departments, vesting powers, and setting up the boards and commissions. Title 2 is the Charter's structure turned into a running administration accountable to the Council and mayor.
Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) — lensUnder the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, government is a trust held for the people; every department and commission exists to serve, not to rule. The lens reads Title 2 as the place to test whether the administration's structure actually delivers aloha in action — accessible boards, accountable departments, and decisions made in the open.
⚖️ Position — analysis, not lawThe org chart is a moral document: it decides who answers to the people. Title 2 should keep departments accountable and commissions accessible — the structure exists to serve residents, full stop. Same title, read two ways.
Free now (no account): this whole page is Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a "which department / which board" router and an AI administration assistant will be verification-gated via the free govOS beta portal (identity verified with Stripe Identity — free, no charge); only verified-status is stored, never the raw ID. No secret keys (environment references only). This completes the plain-language navigators for all 17 Maui County Code titles.
